_readme: Sample scaffold for the AI Platform Engineer take-home. Three Supermetrics customer accounts, three different underlying churn drivers, all with a similar-looking trigger event. Product/connector names reflect real Supermetrics offerings (data source connectors like Google Ads, Facebook Ads, Salesforce; destinations like BigQuery, Looker Studio, Google Sheets, Excel). A candidate whose analysis step just reformats the trigger event will produce near-identical summaries for all three accounts. A candidate doing real analysis will name the distinct driver per account and cite the specific evidence for it. Note: evt_1001 is included twice in trigger_events on purpose, with the same event_id but a later timestamp, simulating a webhook redelivery — your write step should handle this without creating a duplicate CRM record or duplicate alert.

trigger_events._note: Simulated redelivery of evt_1001 (same event_id, retried a few minutes later). Your pipeline should not write a second CRM update or send a second alert for this.

company_profile.name: Northwind Retail Group
company_profile.primary_use_case: Cross-channel paid media reporting for regional marketing teams, rolled up into a global board dashboard.

usage_snippets: Weekly active users in the Supermetrics Hub down from 24 to 6 over the last 8 weeks.
BigQuery scheduled transfers (14 active) reduced from daily to weekly cadence for Google Ads and Facebook Ads on 2026-06-12.
TikTok Ads and Instagram Insights transfers paused entirely as of 2026-06-15 (no data flowing since).
No Looker Studio dashboard views from the regional marketing team in 19 days; previously ~40 views/week.
API token last used 2026-06-28 for a manual query; previously used daily by the analytics team.
Seat login activity: 18 of 22 seats inactive for 30+ days.

recent_support_tickets.subject: Question about BigQuery transfer scheduling options
Clarify field mapping for GA4 connector in Looker Studio

cs_notes: 2026-06-01: Quick check-in call, contact said the analytics team was 'busy with an internal re-org' and would circle back on reducing scheduled transfer frequency.
2026-06-20: No response to two follow-up emails about the reduced transfer cadence.
2026-07-05: Contact's out-of-office auto-reply mentions a new role title; no alternate contact provided.
2026-07-11: LinkedIn shows the regional marketing team headcount has dropped from 9 to 4 since April, consistent with a re-org.

company_profile.name: Bluefin Analytics
company_profile.primary_use_case: Blending paid media spend with Salesforce pipeline data for marketing-sourced revenue reporting, shared with leadership.

usage_snippets: Weekly active users stable at 30-33 over the last 8 weeks — no drop in logins.
Looker Studio dashboard views and Google Sheets refreshes unchanged from prior quarter.
Scheduled transfer volume consistent with historical baseline (6 transfers/day).
Salesforce connector sync success rate dropped from 99% to 71% over the last 3 weeks specifically for the Opportunity object; other objects (Account, Contact) unaffected.

recent_support_tickets.subject: Salesforce connector intermittently failing to sync Opportunity stage field into Looker Studio
Same Opportunity sync issue recurring after suggested fix (field-level permission change)
Data discrepancy: marketing-sourced revenue dashboard shows different Opportunity stages than Salesforce directly, third occurrence
Escalation: leadership asking why Supermetrics dashboard numbers don't match Salesforce reports used in the board deck

cs_notes: 2026-06-25: Customer visibly frustrated on call, said 'we can't trust the numbers anymore, and we present this to the board.'
2026-07-09: Customer asked directly what we're doing to prevent this recurring; no committed fix date given yet.

2026-07-12: Support engineering flagged this as a known intermittent bug in the Salesforce connector's Opportunity stage field mapping, fix targeted for next connector release — not yet communicated to customer.

company_profile.name: Verdant Foods Co
company_profile.primary_use_case: Monthly board-level marketing performance reporting, built and maintained almost entirely by one person (VP Marketing Ops).

usage_snippets: Weekly active users down from 18 to 11, a moderate decline — not a cliff.
Scheduled Google Sheets refreshes (5 active) still running normally on automated jobs — no technical disruption.
Ad-hoc report building and new query creation (non-scheduled) down sharply — from ~40/week to ~5/week since June.
No new dashboards, tabs, or saved queries created since 2026-06-01.
Only 1 of 11 seats has admin-level access to modify scheduled transfers, and that seat belongs to the departed champion.

recent_support_tickets.subject: Adding new team member seats ahead of Q3 planning

cs_notes: 2026-06-18: Primary champion (VP Marketing Ops, Marc Fontaine) confirmed via LinkedIn as having left the company as of June 1.
2026-06-22: Email to champion bounced with an out-of-office/no-longer-with-company auto-reply.
2026-07-02: No replacement contact identified yet; the reporting setup was champion-led and no one else on the account has admin access to modify or troubleshoot it.

2026-07-10: Reached out to a Contact-role user (analyst level) who confirmed 'reports still seem to be running' but said they don't know how to change anything and haven't been told who owns the relationship now.
